patterns_observed: Widespread codebase rebranding requires mass string replacements which are susceptible to breaking compilation if not carefully checked (e.g., package-lock.json).
Renaming the workspace directory during an active runtime session leads to immediate context loss; deferring physical directory renames to manual user action after code changes is a safe pattern.
corrections: Used a recursive Node.js script to perform mass string replacements of 'AgentQ' to 'MidpointX' across the entire repository while avoiding dependency lockfiles.
Renamed specific file names that contained the old brand name.
proposals: Extract application branding strings into a centralized configuration or environment variable map to prevent having 'hardcoded' names deeply embedded in logic (like system prompts) for future pivots.
